creator: roman@xpert.com
text:
The archivers/unarj port doesn't create subdirectories listed in archives,
nor does it handle multi-volume archives making it useful to very limited
degree.
I was facing it when I tried to back up a bunch of old diskettes.
It was nearly unusable in it's current state.
I contacted ache@freebsd.org but had no reply from him since then.

Fix: The RedHat's Linux distribution ships with unarj patched for creating 
directories. (I include the patch).

The multivolume issue was fixed by some other person, and it's hosted here:
http://www.home.unix-ag.org/arne/unarj/
(I attach it as well).

All theses changes were tested by me on multiple archives and were found
to be ok.

Included also patch-aa and patch-ad - for direct inclusion in the ports.
(I merged them).

This is the patch for subdirectory creation:

--------------------------------end of subdir patch---------------------------

----------------------------begin of multivolume patch------------------------

----------------------------end of multivolume patch--------------------------

----------------------------begin of patch-aa --------------------------------
How-To-Repeat: 
Try to extract arj file with directories or try to extract a multivolume
archive.
